planTitle: Separate Local Work Artifacts from Shared Repository

content: The local-vs-shared boundary: `missions/`, `memory/`, `.cosmonauts/`, and `.claude/` are gitignored personal work artifacts. Framework code in `lib/`, `domains/`, `bundled/`, and `cli/` is tracked. `ROADMAP.md` and `AGENTS.md` are framework documentation and also remain tracked — they describe the system, not a user's personal work history.

No runtime code changes were required to establish the local-vs-shared boundary. All file-system operations in `lib/tasks/` already accepted `projectRoot` as a parameter and used `mkdir({ recursive: true })`. The gap was purely at the git/distribution layer — the directories were simply tracked when they shouldn't have been. Existing agent path resolution was already correct.

`ensureForgeDirectory` in `lib/tasks/file-system.ts` creates the full local scaffold via `Promise.all` with `mkdir({ recursive: true })`. The canonical scaffold is: `missions/tasks/`, `missions/plans/`, `missions/archive/tasks/`, `missions/archive/plans/`, `missions/reviews/`, and `memory/`. The function is idempotent — safe to call on existing projects. It never overwrites `missions/tasks/config.json` if it already exists.

`DEFAULT_WORKFLOWS` in `lib/workflows/defaults.ts` is an empty array — the framework ships with no built-in named workflows. Workflows are defined exclusively in the project-local `.cosmonauts/config.json`. Without a config file, `loadWorkflows()` returns `[]` and `resolveWorkflow()` throws for any name. This keeps the framework opinion-free; projects define what they need.

`.cosmonauts/config.example.json` is the canonical, git-tracked workflow template. `.gitignore` uses `.cosmonauts/*` with a `!.cosmonauts/config.example.json` exception to track only the example file. Users copy it to `.cosmonauts/config.json` and customize. The example file is the single source of truth for the standard named workflows (plan-and-build, implement, verify) — not `lib/config/defaults.ts`.

Extending `ensureForgeDirectory` to pre-create `memory/` and `missions/archive/` broke tests in `tests/plans/archive.test.ts`. Those tests had precondition assertions that the directories did not exist before `archivePlan()` was called. Fix: remove stale precondition assertions; instead test that the specific plan is not present in the archive rather than asserting the archive directory doesn't exist.

After adding paths to `.gitignore`, run `git rm --cached -r missions/ memory/ .claude/` to remove them from the git index without deleting local copies. Existing clones will see those paths show up as deleted files on the next `git pull` — this is expected and correct, not a mistake. Document this in the commit message so collaborators are not surprised.

The canonical scaffolding entrypoint is `cosmonauts scaffold missions` (CLI), which calls `TaskManager.init()` → `ensureForgeDirectory`. This is the single command new users run after cloning to create all local directories. It must remain idempotent so it can safely be re-run on existing projects. The `bundled/` directory contains installable domain packages (source of truth for domains); `missions/` holds local active work artifacts; `memory/` holds distilled knowledge — all three are distinct layers with different git tracking status.
